questions.questionText: Describe the steps to construct the perpendicular bisector of a line segment AB using only a ruler and compass.
questions.correctAnswer: Open compass to more than half AB; draw arcs above and below from A and B; join the two intersection points.

markScheme.desc: Open compass to more than half the length of AB
Draw arcs from A and from B intersecting above and below AB
Join the two intersection points to give the perpendicular bisector

workingSteps: 1. Set compass to a radius greater than half of AB.
2. With centre A, draw arcs above and below AB.
3. With the same radius and centre B, draw arcs crossing the first arcs at X and Y.
4. Join X and Y; this line is the perpendicular bisector of AB.
questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector is equidistant from both endpoints.

questions.questionText: Describe how to bisect an angle ABC using only a ruler and compass.
questions.correctAnswer: Arc from B cuts BA and BC; equal arcs from those points intersect; join B to intersection.

markScheme.desc: Draw an arc centred at B cutting BA at P and BC at Q
Draw equal arcs from P and Q intersecting at R
Draw the ray BR — this bisects angle ABC

workingSteps: 1. With centre B, draw an arc that cuts arm BA at P and arm BC at Q.
2. With equal radius, draw arcs from P and Q intersecting at R.
3. Join B to R; ray BR bisects angle ABC.
questions.explanation: The angle bisector is equidistant from both arms of the angle.

questions.context: Triangle ABC has AB = 7 cm, BC = 5 cm and AC = 6 cm.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Describe how to construct triangle ABC accurately using only a ruler and compass.
parts.correctAnswer: Draw AB = 7 cm; arc radius 5 cm from B and arc radius 6 cm from A; intersection is C.

markScheme.desc: Arc of radius 5 cm centred at B
Arc of radius 6 cm centred at A; intersection gives C

workingSteps: 2. Set compass to 5 cm; draw an arc centred at B.
3. Set compass to 6 cm; draw an arc centred at A.
4. The intersection of the two arcs is point C.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
Using your construction, find the perpendicular height from C to AB by construction.
Describe the steps.
parts.correctAnswer: Construct the perpendicular from C to line AB: draw arcs from C cutting AB, then bisect.

markScheme.desc: Draw arc centred at C cutting AB at two points P and Q
Draw equal arcs from P and Q intersecting at R (opposite side from C)
Join C to R; foot of perpendicular is where CR meets AB

workingSteps: 1. With centre C, draw an arc cutting line AB at P and Q.
2. With equal radius, draw arcs from P and Q intersecting at R (below AB).
3. Join C to R; where CR crosses AB is the foot of the perpendicular.
4. The height from C to AB is the length from C to this foot.
